title: Insurance Product (extracted)
description: Rich, source-cited structured representation of one insurance product document (general conditions / IPID / product sheet). Designed to preserve the MAXIMUM of the source PDF's substance. The `branch` value must be one of the slugs declared in the country's sources/<cc>/_country.yml (it is intentionally NOT a fixed global enum, so the schema is country-agnostic). Every substantive block carries a `page` citation into the source PDF.

prompt_version.description: Version of the extraction prompt used (from extraction-agent/). Part of the extraction cache key.

product_name.description: Commercial product name exactly as in the document.

branch.description: Branch slug declared in the country's _country.yml (e.g. 'auto').

branch_code.description: Optional regulatory code (e.g. life '21'/'23'), if the country uses one.

target_audience_note.description: Audience as worded in the document, kept verbatim when it does not map cleanly onto target_audience (e.g. 'Toute personne physique n'ayant pas atteint l'age de 70 ans'). Never invent: leave target_audience null rather than guess a category.

edition_date.description: Edition/version date of the document (YYYY-MM-DD or as printed). CRITICAL for version tracking: the same product is re-issued over time, and an old edition may describe a product no longer sold. Always capture it if the document shows one.

product_family.description: Normalized product line, stripped of edition date and distribution variant (e.g. 'Confort Auto', 'Top Habitation'). Used to group editions/variants/documents of the same product.

variant.description: Distribution channel or formula variant if the doc is one of several parallel versions of the same product (e.g. 'BNPPF', 'courtier', 'Flex Premium').

is_extension.description: true if this document is an OPTION / EXTENSION / rider of a base product rather than a standalone product.

extends.description: If is_extension, the name of the base product this extends/complements (as printed).

superseded.description: Set at build time if a newer edition of the same product+document_type exists. Do not set during extraction.

source_url.description: URL of the public source PDF. CRITICAL: the citation anchor.

source_pdf.description: Local filename of the source PDF.

source_sha256.description: Checksum of the source PDF (from manifest.json).

source_pages.description: Total page count of the source PDF.

summary.description: Neutral 2-4 sentence factual summary of what the product is. No advice.

definitions.description: Contractual definitions (glossary) as stated in the document.

coverages.description: Every guarantee/coverage the document describes. Preserve full descriptions and all limits/conditions.

description.description: What it protects against, in the document's own terms.

is_optional.description: true if an optional add-on, false if included by default.

limits.description: Max insured amount / limit of indemnity, verbatim.

exclusions.description: Everything NOT covered. Capture all of them; they are the most-omitted and most-important part.

applies_to.description: Coverage this exclusion applies to, or 'all'.

obligations.description: Duties of the insured (déclarations, prévention, en cas de sinistre).

timing.description: When it applies (at subscription / during / on claim).

claims_procedure.description: How to declare and handle a claim (steps, deadlines).

special_rights.description: e.g. cancel after a claim / after a premium increase.

premium.description: Premium mechanics if described (indexation, payment terms). Not a price quote.

key_quotes.description: Verbatim quotes worth preserving exactly (checked by verify_grounding.py). Accepts either objects {quote, page} or bare strings; extract.py normalizes strings to objects.

gaps.description: Explicit notes about what could NOT be extracted (scanned pages, ambiguous sections, missing info). Honesty over false completeness.
